name: Fowler State Bank
description_short: Colorado state-chartered community bank serving southeastern Colorado since 1899, specializing in agricultural lending with local decision-making and no corporate oversight.
description_long: Fowler State Bank was established in 1899 and has operated continuously as an independent, community-owned financial institution in Fowler, Colorado for over 125 years. The bank is state-chartered by Colorado, FDIC-insured, and maintains a BBB A+ rating since 2014. Leadership includes Jonathan Fox as President & CEO, who formerly served as Chairman of the Colorado Banking Board, bringing regulatory expertise and community credibility to the organization. The bank offers a comprehensive suite of banking services including personal banking (free checking, savings accounts, CDs, personal loans), business banking (business checking, commercial loans, lines of credit), and specialized agricultural banking. Agricultural services include farm and ranch operating loans for working capital, equipment financing for tractors and irrigation systems, livestock loans, and long-term land purchase financing. Additional services include online and mobile banking, safe deposit boxes, notary services, wire transfers, and phone banking (Dial-A-Bank). Fowler State Bank differentiates itself through deep agricultural expertise developed over 125 years, local ownership with no out-of-state shareholders, and same-day loan decisions made by local officers rather than distant corporate headquarters. The bank emphasizes understanding farm cycles, weather impacts on operations, and multi-generational customer relationships. Staff members live in the community and participate in local commerce, creating accountability and personalized service. The institution explicitly contrasts itself with corporate banking by highlighting that loan approvals happen in Fowler, not Denver or other regional centers. The bank is genuinely suited for agricultural customers and southeastern Colorado residents seeking relationship-based banking with agricultural expertise. However, the single physical location in Fowler may limit accessibility for customers outside the immediate area, and the website does not disclose specific interest rates, loan terms, or fee structures that would allow comparison shopping. The bank's strength lies in specialized agricultural lending rather than broader consumer financial services.
services: Equipment financing for tractors, combines, and irrigation systems
Long-term land purchase and ranch property loans
Commercial loans and lines of credit
Online and mobile banking with bill pay and transfers
Safe deposit boxes, notary services, wire transfers, and Dial-A-Bank phone banking
pros: Community-owned with no out-of-state shareholders making decisions about customer money
Same-day loan approval decisions made locally by officers who live in the community
125+ years of continuous operation and agricultural lending expertise since 1899
No monthly fees on checking accounts
FDIC-insured deposits with Colorado state charter oversight
Mobile banking available on iOS and Android with mobile check deposit capability
Specialized agricultural services including livestock loans, equipment financing, and farm operating loans
cons: Single physical branch location in Fowler limits convenience for customers in other areas
Website does not disclose specific interest rates, APRs, or loan terms for comparison
Limited information about digital banking capabilities compared to larger regional banks
No mention of personal loan amounts, credit requirements, or approval timelines
Agricultural focus may mean limited product depth for non-agricultural customers
best_for: Farmers and ranchers in southeastern Colorado seeking specialized agricultural lending
Multi-generational farm families seeking long-term relationship banking with agricultural expertise
Local Fowler and southeastern Colorado residents seeking community-owned banking without corporate fees
Business owners in agricultural industries needing equipment financing and operating capital
diagnosis: Fowler State Bank is best suited for agricultural customers and southeastern Colorado residents who prioritize relationship-based banking with genuine agricultural expertise over broad product selection or convenience. The main caveat is that the bank operates a single physical location and does not publish pricing or loan terms online, requiring customers to contact the bank directly for comparison or application information.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
